name: Hillsborough County Public Schools
districtFact: Eighth largest school district in the United States, serving the Tampa Bay area with a calendar that uniquely observes local festivals as student holidays.

about.title: About the District
about.content: Hillsborough County Public Schools is the eighth-largest school district in the United States, serving approximately 223,000 students across more than 260 schools in the Tampa Bay area of Florida.

planningTips: Florida State Fair (Feb 12) and Strawberry Festival (Mar 8) are no-school days in Hillsborough County — unique local holidays not observed in other states. Add these to your childcare calendar.
Hillsborough Spring Break (Mar 22–26) aligns with South Florida's major counties in 2026-2027. Florida beaches and theme parks statewide will be at peak demand — book early.

Hurricane season (Aug–Oct) risk in Tampa Bay includes significant surge flooding potential. HCPS banks makeup days, but extended closures can push the last day past May 28. Use flexible booking for late-May travel.
The Gasparilla Pirate Festival (late January Saturday) is not a school holiday but draws 300,000+ people to downtown Tampa. Avoid driving in central Tampa and Bayshore on that Saturday.

districtFaqs.q: Is Hillsborough County Public Schools the same as the University of Tampa or USF?
districtFaqs.a: No. Hillsborough County Public Schools (HCPS) is the K-12 public school system serving Hillsborough County and the Tampa Bay area. The University of Tampa (UT) and the University of South Florida (USF) are separate institutions with their own academic calendars that have no connection to HCPS school dates.

districtFaqs.q: What is the Florida Strawberry Festival non-student day in Hillsborough County?
districtFaqs.a: Hillsborough County Public Schools observes the Florida Strawberry Festival (March 8) as a no-school day for students. The Florida Strawberry Festival is held annually in Plant City, Florida — part of Hillsborough County — and celebrates the local strawberry harvest. This is a uniquely local observance not found in school districts outside the Tampa Bay area. Families relocating from other states should mark this as a student holiday for childcare planning.

districtFaqs.q: Does Hillsborough County observe the Florida State Fair as a school holiday?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Hillsborough County Public Schools observes February 12 as a no-school day tied to the Florida State Fair, which is held annually in Tampa in early-to-mid February. This is a locally recognized holiday not observed in school districts outside the Tampa Bay area. Families should plan childcare for this day.

districtFaqs.q: Does HCPS close school for Gasparilla?
districtFaqs.a: No. The Gasparilla Pirate Festival is not a scheduled school holiday for Hillsborough County Public Schools — classes run as normal. However, the festival takes place on a Saturday in late January and draws over 300,000 people to downtown Tampa, causing major road closures across Bayshore Boulevard, Hyde Park, and downtown streets. Families with Saturday activities near downtown should plan around Gasparilla weekend.

districtFaqs.q: Does HCPS offer enrollment support for military families?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. Hillsborough County Public Schools has established procedures for military family enrollment, including expedited processing of school records and placement for students arriving mid-year on PCS orders. MacDill Air Force Base in south Tampa is home to US Central Command and Special Operations Command, and HCPS serves a significant population of active-duty military and DOD civilian families. Families transferring in should contact HCPS enrollment services before the PCS move date to begin the paperwork process.

districtFaqs.q: Is MacDill Air Force Base within HCPS boundaries?
districtFaqs.a: Yes. MacDill Air Force Base is located on a peninsula in south Tampa within Hillsborough County, and dependent children living on or near the base are served by Hillsborough County Public Schools. MacDill is home to US Central Command (CENTCOM) and US Special Operations Command (USSOCOM), making it one of the most operationally significant bases in the country. HCPS is well-practiced in supporting the frequent mid-year transfers and deployment-related transitions that come with serving this community.

livingHere.intro: Hillsborough County anchors the Tampa Bay metro — a rapidly growing area with a deep Latin heritage, a major military presence, and a civic culture built around sports and local festivals. For families new to the area, several distinctly Tampa features shape how the school calendar intersects with daily life.

highlights.label: Tampa International Airport (TPA)
highlights.detail: Consistently ranked among the most traveler-friendly airports in the US, with direct service to major domestic hubs and select international destinations. Spring break and the week after Memorial Day see high demand — book flights 4–6 weeks out, as TPA's manageable size fills quickly during peak school-break periods.

highlights.label: Gasparilla Pirate Festival
highlights.detail: Held on the final Saturday of January, Gasparilla draws over 300,000 people to downtown Tampa and Bayshore Boulevard for a pirate-themed parade. It's not a school holiday, but downtown and South Tampa roads are effectively gridlocked for most of the day — plan accordingly for any Saturday commitments.

highlights.label: Florida State Fair and Strawberry Festival
highlights.detail: Hillsborough County observes both the Florida State Fair (February 12) and the Florida Strawberry Festival (March 8) as no-school days — holidays found nowhere else in the country. These are firm childcare planning dates for families new to the district, particularly the Strawberry Festival, which surprises most newcomers.

highlights.label: MacDill Air Force Base
highlights.detail: MacDill AFB sits on a peninsula in south Tampa and is home to US Central Command and Special Operations Command. A significant portion of HCPS families are active-duty military or DOD civilians — the school district has established procedures for mid-year enrollment and records transfer to support frequent military relocations.

highlights.label: Ybor City and Latin Heritage
highlights.detail: Tampa's historic Ybor City neighborhood has deep Cuban, Spanish, and Italian immigrant roots dating to the cigar industry of the late 1800s. This cultural identity is embedded in Hillsborough County's civic life and is distinct from Miami's Cuban-American community — newer arrivals will find a different and historically rooted Latino presence.

highlights.label: Gulf Coast Beaches — Nearby but Not in District
highlights.detail: Clearwater Beach and St. Pete Beach are among the best-rated Gulf Coast beaches, but they are in Pinellas County — not Hillsborough. The drive is 30–60 minutes depending on traffic and bridge congestion. Post-school beach trips on Fridays can face significant delays on the Howard Frankland and Courtney Campbell bridges.

highlights.label: Where Families Settle: FishHawk, Westchase, New Tampa
highlights.detail: Families relocating to the Tampa area most commonly land in FishHawk Ranch (southeast Hillsborough, highly rated elementary schools), Westchase (northwest, strong school cluster, quick TPA access), or New Tampa (northeast, newer construction, Hunter's Green and K-Bar Ranch areas). Each corridor has distinct school assignments — verify attendance zones at hillsboroughschools.org before signing a lease.
